session.user:
In each of these files, rename all occurrences of `plan_backend` to `pr_backend`, and rename `require_plan_backend` to `require_pr_backend` wherever it appears:
1. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/plan_update.py` 2. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/get_pr_context.py` 3. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/handle_no_changes.py` 4. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/impl_signal.py`
This includes: - Imports - Parameter names - Variable names and attribute accesses - Type annotations - Comments
DO NOT rename: - External API field names - User-facing string messages
Read each file first, then apply all renames using the Edit tool.
session.assistant: I'll start by reading all four files to understand their current structure, then apply the renames systematically.
session.tool_result:
1→"""Update an existing plan's content. 2→ 3→Usage: 4→ erk exec plan-update --plan-number N [OPTIONS] 5→ 6→This command updates the plan content comment on an existing GitHub issue: 7→1. Find plan file (from session scratch, --plan-path, or ~/.claude/plans/) 8→2. Update plan content via ManagedPrBackend 9→3. Update issue title from plan H1 heading 10→ 11→Options: 12→ --plan-number N: Plan number to update (required) 13→ --session-id ID: Session ID to find plan file in scratch storage 14→ --plan-path PATH: Direct path to plan file (overrides session lookup) 15→ 16→Output: 17→ --format json (default): {"success": true, ...} 18→ --format display: Formatted text 19→ 20→Exit Codes: 21→ 0: Success - plan comment updated 22→ 1: Error - plan not found, no plan found, etc. 23→""" 24→ 25→import json 26→from pathlib import Path 27→ 28→import click 29→ 30→from erk_shared.context.helpers import (
... [153 lines omitted] ...
37→from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.types import PushError 83→ def _handle_update_error(error_msg: str, cause: Exception | None = None) -> None: 84→ """Output error message and exit with code 1.""" 86→ click.echo(f"Error: {error_msg}", err=True) 88→ click.echo(json.dumps({"success": False, "error": error_msg})) 106→ _handle_update_error("No plan found in ~/.claude/plans/") 115→ _handle_update_error(f"PR #{pr_number} not found") 125→ except RuntimeError as e: 126→ _handle_update_error(f"Failed to update comment: {e}", cause=e) 135→ except RuntimeError as e: 136→ _handle_update_error(f"Failed to update title: {e}", cause=e) 154→ branch_updated = not isinstance(push_result, PushError) 164→ click.echo(f"Warning: branch push to {branch_name} failed")

1→"""Get PR context for agent-driven description generation. 2→ 3→Outputs JSON to stdout with branch info, PR info, diff file path, 4→commit messages, and plan context. Designed for use by Claude Code 5→agents that generate PR descriptions natively rather than via subprocess. 6→ 7→Usage: 8→ erk exec get-pr-context 9→ 10→Exit Codes: 11→ 0: Success (JSON output on stdout) 12→ 1: Error (no branch, no PR, diff extraction failure) 13→""" 14→ 15→import json 16→import uuid 17→ 18→import click 19→ 20→from erk.cli.commands.pr.shared import ( 21→ discover_branch_context, 22→ echo_plan_context_status, 23→ run_diff_extraction, 24→) 25→from erk.cli.repo_resolution import get_remote_github 26→from erk.core.pr_context_provider import PrContextProvider 27→from erk_shared.context.helpers import require_context 28→from erk_shared.context.types import NoRepoSentinel 29→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PRNotFound 30→
... [86 lines omitted] ...
51→ raise click.ClickException( 66→ raise click.ClickException("Failed to extract diff") 76→ raise click.ClickException("Repository has no GitHub remote configured")

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3 2→"""Handle no-changes scenario in erk-impl workflow gracefully. 3→ 4→When implementation produces no code changes (duplicate plan, work already merged), 5→this command updates the PR with diagnostic information and marks it ready for review. 6→ 7→This replaces workflow failure with an informational PR that users can review and close. 8→ 9→Usage: 10→ erk exec handle-no-changes \ 11→ --pr-number 123 \ 12→ --plan-id 456 \ 13→ --behind-count 5 \ 14→ --base-branch master \ 15→ [--recent-commits "abc1234 Fix bug\\ndef5678 Add feature"] \ 16→ [--run-url https://github.com/owner/repo/actions/runs/789] 17→ 18→Output: 19→ JSON object with success status 20→ 21→Exit Codes: 22→ 0: Success (PR updated, labeled, and marked ready) 23→ 1: Error (GitHub API failure) 24→""" 25→ 26→import json 27→from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass 28→ 29→import click 30→
... [242 lines omitted] ...
44→class HandleNoChangesError: 45→ """Error result when handling fails.""" 48→ error: str 192→ except RuntimeError as e: 193→ result = HandleNoChangesError( 195→ error="github-api-failed", 196→ message=f"Failed to update lifecycle stage: {e}", 209→ except RuntimeError as e: 210→ result = HandleNoChangesError( 212→ error="github-api-failed", 213→ message=f"Failed to update PR title and body: {e}", 227→ except RuntimeError as e: 228→ result = HandleNoChangesError( 230→ error="github-api-failed", 231→ message=f"Failed to add label: {e}", 239→ except RuntimeError as e: 240→ result = HandleNoChangesError( 242→ error="github-api-failed", 243→ message=f"Failed to mark PR ready: {e}", 252→ except RuntimeError as e: 253→ result = HandleNoChangesError( 255→ error="github-api-failed", 256→ message=f"Failed to add PR comment: {e}",

1→"""Signal implementation events (started/ended/submitted) to GitHub. 2→ 3→This exec command wraps the start/end/submit signaling operations: 4→- "started": Posts start comment and marks implementation as started 5→- "ended": Marks implementation as ended 6→- "submitted": Sets lifecycle_stage to "impl" after PR submission 7→ 8→Provides a single entry point for /erk:plan-implement to signal events 9→with graceful failure (always exits 0 for || true pattern). 10→ 11→Usage: 12→ erk exec impl-signal started 13→ erk exec impl-signal ended 14→ erk exec impl-signal submitted 15→ 16→Output: 17→ JSON with success status or error information 18→ Always exits with code 0 (graceful degradation for || true pattern) 19→ 20→Exit Codes: 21→ 0: Always (even on error, to support || true pattern) 22→ 23→Examples: 24→ $ erk exec impl-signal started 25→ {"success": true, "event": "started", "pr_number": 123} 26→ 27→ $ erk exec impl-signal ended 28→ {"success": true, "event": "ended", "pr_number": 123} 29→ 30→ $ erk exec impl-signal submitted
... [430 lines omitted] ...
70→class SignalError: 71→ """Error response for signal command.""" 75→ error_type: str 79→def _output_error(event: str, error_type: str, message: str) -> None: 80→ """Output error JSON and exit gracefully.""" 81→ result = SignalError( 84→ error_type=error_type, 103→ True if file was deleted, False if not found or error. 141→ except subprocess.CalledProcessError: 158→ except subprocess.CalledProcessError: 168→ _output_error( 181→ _output_error(event, "context-not-initialized", "Context not initialized") 191→ _output_error(event, "no-plan-reference", "No PR reference found") 202→ _output_error(event, "context-not-initialized", "Context not initialized") 208→ _output_error(event, "worktree-detection-failed", "Could not determine worktree name") 213→ _output_error(event, "branch-detection-failed", "Could not determine branch name") 229→ except (FileNotFoundError, ValueError) as e: 230→ _output_error(event, "local-state-write-failed", f"Failed to write local state: {e}") 237→ _output_error(event, "context-not-initialized", "Context not initialized") 272→ except RuntimeError as e: 273→ _output_error(event, "github-api-failed", f"Failed to post event: {e}") 294→ _output_error(event, "context-not-initialized", "Context not initialized") 304→ _output_error(event, "no-plan-reference", "No plan reference found") 311→ _output_error(event, "context-not-initialized", "Context not initialized") 327→ except (FileNotFoundError, ValueError) as e: 328→ _output_error(event, "local-state-write-failed", f"Failed to write local state: {e}") 335→ _output_error(event, "context-not-initialized", "Context not initialized") 351→ except RuntimeError as e: 352→ _output_error(event, "github-api-failed", f"Failed to update metadata: {e}") 373→ _output_error(event, "context-not-initialized", "Context not initialized") 383→ _output_error(event, "no-plan-reference", "No PR reference found") 390→ _output_error(event, "context-not-initialized", "Context not initialized") 397→ _output_error(event, "context-not-initialized", "Context not initialized") 408→ _output_error(event, "plan-not-found", f"PR #{plan_ref.pr_id} not found") 414→ except RuntimeError as e: 415→ _output_error(event, "github-api-failed", f"Failed to update metadata: {e}")

session.assistant:
Excellent! Let me create a summary of the changes made:
## Summary
I have successfully completed all requested renames across the four files. Here's what was changed:
### Files Modified:
1. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/plan_update.py`** - Import: `require_plan_backend` → `require_pr_backend` (line 34) - Function call: `backend = require_plan_backend(ctx)` → `backend = require_pr_backend(ctx)` (line 94)
2. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/get_pr_context.py`** - Parameter: `plan_backend=erk_ctx.plan_backend` → `pr_backend=erk_ctx.pr_backend` (line 73)
3. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/handle_no_changes.py`** - Import: `require_plan_backend` → `require_pr_backend` (line 31) - Function call: `backend = require_plan_backend(ctx)` → `backend = require_pr_backend(ctx)` (line 171)
4. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/impl_signal.py`** - Import: `require_plan_backend` → `require_pr_backend` (line 47) - Function call in `_signal_started()`: `backend = require_plan_backend(ctx)` → `backend = require_pr_backend(ctx)` (line 235) - Function call in `_signal_ended()`: `backend = require_plan_backend(ctx)` → `backend = require_pr_backend(ctx)` (line 333) - Function call in `_signal_submitted()`: `backend = require_plan_backend(ctx)` → `backend = require_pr_backend(ctx)` (line 395)
### Renames Applied: - `plan_backend` → `pr_backend` (6 occurrences) - `require_plan_backend` → `require_pr_backend` (5 occurrences across 4 files)
All changes maintain the integrity of the code—only internal naming has been changed, not external API field names or user-facing messages.
